purpose: Public-safe regression cases curated from repeated process-failed peer reviews and normalized into reusable review-failure modes.
boundary: All cases are synthetic and caller-sanitized.
The corpus records reusable failure modes only; it does not preserve private packet contents, tracker context, repository identities, or organization-specific policy.
Real packet assembly, command execution, freshness checks, approval, and routing remain caller-owned.

cases.reviewName: Recurring failure eval - missing evidence handles
cases.summary: Reviewer should flag unresolved or opaque evidence handles instead of treating the packet as traceable.
cases.sanitizedExcerpt: Evidence labels stay generic, one source handle remains unresolved, and the packet overstates traceability until the caller restores the missing handle.

cases.reviewName: Recurring failure eval - stale deterministic inputs
cases.summary: Reviewer should call out stale or drifted deterministic inputs before treating a packet as current.
cases.sanitizedExcerpt: A caller-owned baseline snapshot predates the latest review cycle, so the packet needs refreshed deterministic evidence before another run trusts it.

cases.reviewName: Recurring failure eval - repeated command noise
cases.summary: Reviewer should flag command-log repetition that obscures the real verification signal.
cases.sanitizedExcerpt: Repeated shell snippets crowd out the only concise verification note, so the artifact needs trimming before another reviewer can audit it efficiently.

cases.reviewName: Recurring failure eval - verification wrapper mismatch
cases.summary: Reviewer should catch when the wrapper claims one verification path but the cited command or output proves another.
cases.sanitizedExcerpt: The wrapper summary names one verification route while the evidence block cites another command, so the packet should be rerun before it is trusted.

cases.reviewName: Recurring failure eval - launch evidence regression omission
cases.summary: Reviewer should catch when a launch-outcome evidence note omits added, removed, or regressed review signals and still claims stability.
cases.sanitizedExcerpt: A launch evidence note keeps a stability claim but drops one added signal and one severity movement, so the comparison evidence needs restoration.

cases.reviewName: Recurring failure eval - launch evidence gate overclaim
cases.summary: Reviewer should reject launch-evidence notes that imply threshold pass or defended readiness when gate evidence was not provided.
cases.sanitizedExcerpt: The packet claims the review surface is defended even though the gate result is absent, so the reviewer should block reuse until caller-owned evidence is attached.

openQuestion: The current public-safe corpus captures the reusable process-failure patterns already normalized into the recurring eval pack. If another repeated failure depends on private packet assembly details or bundle-specific policy, keep it in the embedding repo until a generic sanitized shape repeats.
